# Extracao: transcription_mp4_3__The_End_Game_is_the_Close

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_3__The_End_Game_is_the_Close.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

You know, the world is littered with the bodies of salespeople who may have learned how to sell but failed miserably when it came time to actually close the sale. There are even more people, billions in fact, who have had great dreams, incredible dreams, ideas of phenomenal products that ended up giving up on those ideas because they couldn't convince close others on supporting them in making those dreams become reality. Thus the dream dies and it's forgotten until someone later picks up that same idea, concept, product, that dream and is able to close others successfully to support that idea or dream or product, whatever and get acceptance in the marketplace and actually make that dream a reality. Well, who gets credit? The dreamer or the closer? See, what we want to do here is explain to you how to make anything happen and close. It's called the critical exchange point. The close is the critical exchange point. The closing is that critical exchange point whereby there's a transfer point from one action to another. You understand? The customer's gone from thinking, talking, negotiating to doing. This is where you quit selling and you get the buyer to take action. Where they exchange something they have for something you have. The close unlike selling is a specific moment as opposed to a series of things. The close is a specific moment where you acquire an agreement and all parties actually take an action. Not just agree but actually take an action or actions and things of value are exchanged. If there is no exchange and no action, trust me, there will be no close. A lot of sales people miss this point. If there is no close, no agreement, then there is no real value exchanged. Do you understand? If you don't close, if you don't exchange products, they exchange something of value with you, there is no real value exchanged. The close is where you, the salesperson or the manager or the dreamer or the entrepreneur, the inventor, whatever you are for the first time in your negotiations become of real value and actually benefit your prospect. That may seem harsh to you. That may seem unreal but the reality is until exchange takes place, until you give me something and I give you something, I can't really be of service because I haven't closed and I haven't created an exchange, you haven't really created value. Until the close, the agreement and exchange takes place, there is no real value in what preceded it. Most of you are stuck in this sales process. I want you to transfer into the exchange. Remember, no close means no exchange. No exchange means no real value. This single misunderstanding is the cause of an individual's company and their dreamers undoing. The failure to close on this critical exchange point explains why targets are not achieved, why your goals are not attained and why dreams are not fulfilled. Look, does any dream, any idea, any invention or concept have any value if it doesn't become a reality? No. It has to become reality which means you have to get to the critical exchange point. This may be a segment you want to watch a second time, a third time, a fourth time to really get it. You have to get to the critical exchange point.
